text.title: ai-spatial-computing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-spatial-computing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using cloud AI for real-time spatial interactions requiring <20ms latency.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cloud round-trips cannot meet the 20ms comfort threshold for XR. All user-facing AI in spatial environments must run on-device. Cloud AI is only acceptable for non-real-time features (VPAT drafting, documentation generation).

failure-mode.description: Designing gesture interactions without testing with users who have motor differences.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Gesture prediction models are trained on majority-population hand shapes. Users with motor differences, missing fingers, or limb differences experience significantly higher error rates. This is not solvable by AI alone — it requires inclusive hardware design and configurable gesture sensitivity.

failure-mode.description: Deploying gaze-based selection without testing on users who wear glasses or have nystagmus.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Eye tracking calibration fails for common conditions. AI cannot compensate for poor calibration. Gaze-based interactions must always have a non-gaze fallback.

failure-mode.description: Agent XAUR gap analysis presented as a compliance verdict without human expert validation on actual hardware.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: XAUR is not machine-checkable. Agent outputs are a structured starting point. Spatial accessibility assessment requires testing with people with disabilities on actual XR hardware — no agent can substitute.

failure-mode.description: AI companion or avatar features deployed in high-stakes contexts (healthcare navigation, financial guidance) without expert validation of every AI-generated cue.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hallucination risk is high for XR accessibility — this is a thin domain in LLM training data. XAUR references generated by agents must be cross-checked against the actual W3C spec.

failure-mode.description: Recommending on-device AI capabilities without accounting for the constrained compute envelope of target hardware.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Quest 3 and Vision Pro have very different compute budgets. Agent recommendations must account for the specific device's thermal, battery, and inference constraints — cloud-quality AI is not available on most current XR hardware.
